headline: One year from presidential election, a 'quiet anger' in France's streets
description: Stagnant disposable income, soaring oil and gas prices, and the looming threat of inflation weigh heavily over this year's May Day. Still, resentment remains muted.

headline: 'Writing helps us organize our ideas. How will we understand the world if we let AI do the writing?'
London antisemitic attack alarms European intelligence services
US warns allies over support to Gaza aid flotilla 'stunt'
description: The Global Sumud Flotilla, which aims to break through the Israeli blockade of the Gaza Strip, claimed 211 of its activists had been 'kidnapped' by Israel. Israel said it had arrested around 175 activists. Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu called the interception a 'total success,' and described participants as 'Hamas supporters.'

headline: European Central Bank moves toward a June interest rate hike
description: The European Central Bank (ECB) kept its main policy rate unchanged on Thursday, but made it clear that action would be needed to 'bring inflation back to 2%.'

headline: 'Céline Dion will generate the equivalent of one-fifth of the economic impact of the Paris Olympics'
description: The singer's series of concerts scheduled for this fall could bring up to €1 billion to the French economy, writes Le Monde journalist Béatrice Madeline in her column.

headline: EU-Mercosur trade deal enters into force: Five questions about where things stand
description: Despite opposition from France, the trade agreement is being provisionally applied as of May 1. Its final entry into force still depends on a vote by the European Parliament.

In a small French village, a father is charged with sexual abuse of 34 victims aged 3 to 9
description: A tree, a rope, a letter. After surviving a suicide attempt, a man revealed he had committed numerous crimes against children. After a year-long and controversial investigation, he was charged, on April 10, with rape and sexual assault of minors and recording child pornography.
byline: Jérémie Pham-Lê and  Lorraine de Foucher

headline: 'In the war between the US and Iran, both sides are convinced they control the clock'
description: Tehran believes Trump needs a deal; Washington thinks the Islamic Republic cannot survive without one, explains Alain Frachon in his column for Le Monde.

headline: The French love their public libraries. But lack of resources undermines the experience
description: France's unique network of 15,500 local cultural spaces, which are open to all, free of charge, has seen visitor numbers steadily increase, leading to certain issues.
